creator: theraven@FreeBSD.org
text:
On 2 Sep 2013, at 13:00, Anton Shterenlikht <mexas@bris.ac.uk> wrote:

> I only send this PR because it is a regression:
> 
> # pkg info -xd gnustep
> gnustep-make-2.6.2:
>        mpfr-3.1.2
>        gmp-5.1.2
>        gcc-4.2.5.20090325_5
>        libiconv-1.14_1
> # 
> 
> Have things changed dramatically between 2.6.2 and 2.6.5?

Using gcc with GNUstep was discouraged long before 2.6.2 and a number of features were disabled in our port because of the use of legacy compilers.  GCC's Objective-C support is about 10 years behind the current version of the language.

> Given that LLVM on ia64 is not going to work any time soon,
> if ever, am I right in assuming that gnustep will not build
> from now on? If so, are there any alternatives?
> 
> Anyway, perhaps you should mark this port as NOT_FOR_ARCHS=ia64

It's implicitly NOT_FOR_ARCHS={anything llvm33 or one of its other dependencies doesn't work on}.  

David
